summary: Defer LOAD-TIME-VALUE evaluation to load time under COMPILE-FILE via a memoized synthesized form, keeping the eager path for REPL/LOAD.

items.title: compile_load_time_value: defer evaluation under COMPILE-FILE
items.description:
Make LOAD-TIME-VALUE conformant under COMPILE-FILE (CLHS: evaluated at load time in a null lexical environment), while preserving the eager REPL/LOAD path.

IMPLEMENTATION:

1) New global flag indicating COMPILE-FILE is active.
   - src/core/compiler_internal.h: declare `extern int cl_compiling_to_file;` (place near other compiler-state globals; group with the existing compiler externs).
   - src/core/compiler_extra.c (or compiler.c, wherever a definition-site fits cleanly): `int cl_compiling_to_file = 0;`.
   - src/core/builtins_io.c — `bi_compile_file` (around the start of the read-compile loop, src/core/builtins_io.c:1506-1568): save the previous value of `cl_compiling_to_file`, set it to 1 for the duration of the loop, and restore it before returning (including the early-return paths inside CL_CATCH/CL_ERR_EXIT — use a single save site high in the function and restore in every exit path, OR wrap the read-compile loop in a save/restore block. Nesting-safe.) Take care that the flag is restored on every exit including the CL_ERR_EXIT propagation branch at builtins_io.c:1556-1561.

2) Branch in `compile_load_time_value` (src/core/compiler_extra.c:995).
   - Flag OFF (REPL/LOAD): keep the current behavior unchanged — eager `cl_compile` + `cl_vm_eval` + `cl_emit_const`. This preserves tests/test_vm.c test_eval_load_time_value_* (which depend on side-effects-at-compile-time, since they run in the single-pass eval path).
   - Flag ON (COMPILE-FILE): build a synthesized lazy-memoized form and compile it via `compile_expr`, so the value-form runs at load time and is then cached for subsequent calls.

   The synthesized form (where <CELL> is a freshly-allocated (NIL . NIL) cons unique to this LTV occurrence, and G is a gensym from cl_gensym_with_name("LTV")):

     (LET ((G (QUOTE <CELL>)))
       (IF (CAR G)
           (CDR G)
           (PROGN (RPLACD G <value-form>)
                  (RPLACA G T)
                  (CDR G))))

   Notes:
   - `<CELL>` must be allocated fresh per call to compile_load_time_value so two distinct LTV forms get independent memo cells (no cross-contamination). cl_add_constant dedups by eq, so distinct cons cells survive the constants table; FASL writes them as distinct OBJ_DEF objects.
   - Assemble the form with nested `cl_cons` and intern symbols (LET, IF, CAR, CDR, PROGN, RPLACA, RPLACD) via `cl_intern_in("...", N, cl_package_cl)` — QUOTE is available as SYM_QUOTE. Mirror the style in compile_extra.c's qq_quote/qq_list helpers (compiler_extra.c:212-231).
   - GC SAFETY (CRITICAL — see CLAUDE.md GC Safety section): every CL_Obj local that holds a partially-built form across cl_cons calls (form, value_form, cell, gensym, quoted_cell, body, let_form, intermediate spine cells) MUST be CL_GC_PROTECT'd, with a matching CL_GC_UNPROTECT before returning. The compactor will move arena-relative offsets otherwise.
   - Then call `compile_expr(c, let_form)` exactly as before — no new opcodes needed.

3) FASL versioning.
   - Verify against src/core/fasl.h:29 (CL_FASL_VERSION). This change does NOT alter the FASL wire format (no new tags, no new opcodes, no new constant-table encoding — only the *content* of the constants table differs). Do NOT bump CL_FASL_VERSION.

4) Tests — host (extend tests/test_fasl.c, in the `compile-file and load tests` section starting at line 1803, following the existing TEST() pattern at compile_file_basic etc.):

   a) TEST(compile_file_ltv_forward_function): write a temp .lisp with
        (defun gen-table () (list 1 2 3))
        (defun get-table () (load-time-value (gen-table)))
      Compile-file (must NOT signal Undefined function), load FASL, ASSERT (get-table) => (1 2 3). This is the deflate.lisp regression.

   b) TEST(compile_file_ltv_eq_stable): defun returning (load-time-value (cons 1 2)). Compile-file, load, ASSERT (eq (f) (f)) => T.

   c) TEST(compile_file_ltv_distinct_forms): two defuns, each with its own load-time-value of (cons 'a 'b). Compile-file, load, ASSERT (eq (f1) (f2)) => NIL but each (eq (fN) (fN)) => T. Verifies no eq-dedup cross-contamination (FASL OBJ_DEF mechanism + per-occurrence cell allocation).

   d) TEST(compile_file_ltv_runs_at_load_time): defun whose LTV references a counter incremented at compile time. Compile-file, then verify the counter is NOT incremented by compile-file alone (deferred), then load the FASL and call the function — counter increments exactly once.

   Register all new tests in the test runner table at the bottom of test_fasl.c. Clean up temp .lisp/.fasl files (use the existing write_test_file / delete_cached_fasl helpers, tests/test_fasl.c:1811,1828).

5) Tests — Amiga (extend tests/amiga/run-tests.lisp, in the `FASL compile-file and load` section starting at line 4062, following the existing `(with-open-file ... (compile-file ...) (load ...) (check ...))` idiom):

   - One check mirroring the forward-function case (the deflate.lisp regression — write a file where load-time-value calls a function defun'd earlier in the same file, compile, load, verify result).
   - One check that (eq (f) (f)) => T for an LTV returning a fresh cons.
   - One check that two distinct LTV forms return non-eq independent objects.

6) Keep existing tests passing:
   - tests/test_vm.c test_eval_load_time_value_basic, _in_function, _with_read_only, _null_lex_env, _side_effect, _complex_form, _nested all run via eval_print which uses the REPL path — cl_compiling_to_file stays 0, so eager evaluation continues to apply.
   - The existing Amiga ltv checks at run-tests.lisp:687-694 likewise run in the eval path.

7) Verification: run `make test` and `make test-plus`; both must pass. test-plus exercises host-cold-test which loads quicklisp/deflate.lisp — this is the original failing path.

AFFECTED FILES (each is read for context around one specific region — no full-file reads required):
  - src/core/compiler_extra.c (modify compile_load_time_value at line 995; reference qq_quote/qq_list helpers at 212-231 for the assembly pattern)
  - src/core/builtins_io.c (set/restore flag around bi_compile_file's read-compile loop, lines ~1500-1570)
  - src/core/compiler_internal.h (one extern declaration)
  - tests/test_fasl.c (add 4 TEST functions in the compile-file section ~line 1800+, register in runner)
  - tests/amiga/run-tests.lisp (add 3 checks in the FASL section ~line 4062+)
